card_description: Humans is a historical figure referenced in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. Appears in 2 Exegesis passages. Context: "Dick analyzes how our sense of causality is manipulated by a higher intelligence (Noos)."

linked_segments.summary: Dick analyzes how our sense of causality is manipulated by a higher intelligence (Noos). He suggests that what we perceive as "cause and effect" is often a concealment used by Zebra to move the world
linked_segments.title: 1976-09-15_Dorothy_114.txt

linked_segments.summary: Dick suggests that the artifact (Zebra) projects our reality so that the Urgrund can achieve self-awareness. By observing the "shadows" it projects onto the material plane, the primordial ground learn
linked_segments.title: 1976-09-15_Dorothy_110.txt
